LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FROM PLAINTIFF TO DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, SET ONE. TRANSFERRED TO BE HEARD ON THE COURT'S DISCOVERY CALENDAR (DEPT. 301) ON NOVEMBER 1, 2023 AT 9:00 A.M. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES FROM PLAINTIFF TO DEFENDANTS REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, SET ONE IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL WITHIN 15 DAYS PRODUCED A VERIFIED RESPONSE TO THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION INCLUDING ALL PAGES OF THE REQUESTED DOCUMENTS-FRONT AND BACK. NO SANCTIONS. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR FULL DETAILS) THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302 /JPT)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-20-2025 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-22-2024 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Apr-15-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration
Off Calendar Nov-19-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA's Motion To Compel Binding Arbitration.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is off calendar. Notice of settlement of entire case filed November 15, 2024.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-27-2023 Set For Trial On Dec-09-24 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses From Plaintiff To Defendants Request For Production, Set One.
Off Calendar Nov-01-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Elham Hassantash, Esq., THETA LAW FIRM, LLP (424) 297-3103 eservice@thetafirm.com Attorneys for Defendant Hyundai Motor Americafor Defendant.
No other appearances.

No discussion or arguement is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA's Motion To Compel Further Responses From Plaintiff To Defendants Request For Production, Set One.

The Motion is granted. Plaintiff shall within 15 days produced a verified response to the request for production including all pages of the requested documents-front and back.

No sanctions. Where monetary sanctions are sought, the "notice of motion shall be supported by a memorandum of points and authorities, and accompanied by a declaration setting forth facts supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought." Cal. Civ. Proc. Code 2023.040. Defendants failed to provide evidence of hours worked and billing rate in support of its motion.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)
